#.description: A line starting with # is a comment. Comments are ignored by the pattern matcher.

*.description: Matches any number of characters except the path separator /. Used in glob patterns like *.log or src/*.

**.description: Matches zero or more directories. Can be used as a leading path (e.g. **/foo), trailing path (e.g. abc/**), or in the middle (e.g. a/**/b).

?.description: Matches exactly one character except the path separator /.

!.description: Negates a pattern so that a matching file excluded by a previous pattern becomes included again. It is not possible to re-include a file if a parent directory of that file is excluded.

/.description: A leading / anchors the pattern to the directory of the ignore file. A trailing / means the pattern should only match directories.

[.description: Matches a single character from the set enclosed in brackets. Supports ranges like [a-z] and negation like [!abc].

node_modules.description: Node.js dependency directory. Should almost always be ignored in JavaScript/TypeScript projects.

.env.description: Environment variable configuration file, typically containing secrets and sensitive configuration. Should be ignored to prevent credential leaks.

.DS_Store.description: macOS Finder metadata file that stores custom attributes of folders. Should be ignored cross-platform.

Thumbs.db.description: Windows Explorer thumbnail cache database file. Should be ignored cross-platform.

__pycache__.description: Python bytecode cache directory created by the Python interpreter.

dist.description: Distribution/build output directory. Common in JavaScript, TypeScript, and many other project types.

build.description: Build output directory used by many build systems and frameworks.

coverage.description: Test coverage report output directory generated by testing frameworks.

.git.description: Git version control directory. Commonly ignored in .dockerignore files.

vendor.description: Vendored dependency directory used in Go, PHP (Composer), and Ruby (Bundler) projects.

.vscode.description: Visual Studio Code workspace configuration directory.

target.description: Build output directory used by Rust (Cargo), Java (Maven), and other build systems.
